meta.title: Spec stronger markdown crawling
meta.summary: Hubble Desktop moves markdown discovery into a main-process workspace file index so opening a large folder keeps the editor shell usable while the sidebar indexes incrementally, respecting ignore files. Use the four views to get oriented, then follow data, code, and user-action flow.

graphs.label: System overview
graphs.summary: Stable architecture of the Hubble Desktop workspace file subsystem: where filesystem access lives, how the renderer reaches it, and where workspace state and the sidebar surface are owned.

nodes.title: Electron main: filesystem owner
nodes.summary: The Electron main process is the only code with direct filesystem access. It validates granted roots, lists directories, reads and writes files, and runs chokidar watchers. Renderer code never touches the disk directly, so all discovery and watching is centralized here behind grant checks.
details: Main owns grants, directory listing, file IO, and watchers.
ADR-0002 keeps filesystem access in Electron main behind a typed bridge.

nodes.title: Typed desktop bridge
nodes.summary: A preload script exposes a single typed desktopApi object over contextBridge, and a shared types module defines its contract. The renderer calls main through request/response invokes and subscribes to push channels for streaming updates, giving every renderer feature one strongly-typed surface to the OS.
details: Renderer-to-main calls go through invoke; live updates use subscribe channels.
One typed contract is shared by preload and renderer.

nodes.title: Renderer workspace store
nodes.summary: The renderer keeps workspace and document state in a small store updated through reducer-style actions. It tracks the open workspace path, the visible file list, and the active document separately. Persistence saves the workspace path and last-opened files, but treats the file list as rebuildable rather than durable state.
details: Actions mutate workspace and document state with workspace-path guards.
ADR-0001 treats workspace loading as an atomic snapshot; the file list is not persisted.

nodes.title: Sidebar navigation surface
nodes.summary: The desktop Sidebar adapts workspace store state into the shared UI sidebar component. It renders the markdown file tree plus empty, loading, and error affordances. Navigation in the sidebar is separate from document loading, so the editor can show a file that is not currently selected in the tree.
details: Sidebar maps workspace files into the shared @hubble.md/ui sidebar.
Document loading is independent of which sidebar row is selected.

tour.title: Where files live
tour.body: Start at the Electron main process: it is the single owner of filesystem access, so all discovery and watching is centralized behind grant checks.

tour.title: How the renderer reaches files
tour.body: The renderer never touches disk directly. It goes through a typed preload bridge using request/response invokes and subscription channels.

tour.title: Where workspace state lives
tour.body: Renderer state for the open workspace and active document is held in a small store, mutated through reducer-style actions, with the file list treated as rebuildable.

tour.title: What the user sees
tour.body: The sidebar surface adapts store state into the shared sidebar component and keeps file navigation separate from document loading.

graphs.label: Data flow graph
graphs.summary: How markdown file state moves: spec intent shapes a main-process index that crawls and watches the folder, streams typed events over IPC, reduces into renderer state, and renders the sidebar.

nodes.title: Spec intent
nodes.summary: Keep the shell usable; index the sidebar incrementally and ignore-aware.
details: PRODUCT.md: open folder enters immediately, sidebar shows an indexing state, ignored files stay openable.
TECH.md: move incremental crawl ownership into a main-process indexer instead of full refreshes.
No human or agent review comments yet; only an automated walkthrough-preview bot comment exists on the PR.

nodes.title: Workspace file index
nodes.summary: Main-process index crawls markdown, watches, and emits typed deltas.
details: createWorkspaceFileIndex performs an ignore-aware crawl, then watches with chokidar.
Emits indexing -> ready(snapshot) -> update(upsert/remove) and error events, with debounced batching (~100ms).
Ignore-config edits rebuild rules and recrawl; hard-ignores .git, dist, node_modules.

nodes.title: IPC event stream
nodes.summary: desktop:watch-workspace-files streams index events to the renderer.
details: main.ts registers watch/unwatch handlers and forwards each event on a per-watchId channel.
preload.watchWorkspaceFiles subscribes and returns an unsubscribe that also closes the main-side index.
Per-watchId routing guards against stale subscriptions updating a new workspace.

nodes.title: Renderer state reducer
nodes.summary: applyWorkspaceFileIndexEvent folds events into files + fileIndexStatus.
details: indexing sets status; ready replaces files; update applies upsert/remove into a path-keyed map.
Every branch bails out unless event workspacePath still matches state.workspacePath (latest-wins guard).
Index removals never close the editor or clear last-opened paths.

nodes.title: Sidebar render
nodes.summary: Sidebar shows indexing / error / file tree from fileIndexStatus.
details: While indexing, the sidebar shows a compact status instead of a partial tree.
On ready, it renders the markdown-only tree; on error it shows a recoverable message.
currentPath may be absent from files, so an explicitly opened ignored file keeps the editor open.

tour.title: Start with intent
tour.body: The specs and ADR-0008 define the goal: enter the workspace immediately and index the sidebar incrementally while respecting ignore files.

tour.title: Index produces data
tour.body: The main-process index crawls markdown once, then watches and emits typed indexing/ready/update/error deltas instead of full refreshes.

tour.title: Cross the process boundary
tour.body: Each event is forwarded over a per-watchId IPC channel and delivered through the preload subscription, which can also tear the index down.

tour.title: Reduce into state
tour.body: applyWorkspaceFileIndexEvent folds events into the file list and fileIndexStatus, guarded so stale events cannot mutate a newly opened workspace.

tour.title: Render the result
tour.body: The sidebar reads fileIndexStatus to show indexing, error, or the final markdown tree, keeping the editor usable throughout.

graphs.label: Code dependency graph
graphs.summary: Which changed modules depend on each other: renderer and main entry points at the top, the shared event-type seam in the middle, and ignore/markdown rules as the leaf dependency.

nodes.title: App.tsx (renderer entry)
nodes.summary: useEffect subscribes watchWorkspaceFiles and forwards events to the reducer.
details: Replaces the old refreshFiles-on-every-watcher-event effect with a workspace-index subscription.
Active-file watcher (watchPath) for external-change conflicts is kept separate.

nodes.title: main.ts (IPC entry)
nodes.summary: Registers watch/unwatch IPC and constructs an index per watchId.
details: Removed the inline ignore/crawl helpers from main and delegated to fileIndex/fileRules.
Wires HUBBLE_DESKTOP_DEBUG_WATCHERS diagnostics into onDebug.

nodes.title: desktopApi/types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines WorkspaceFileIndexEvent and watchWorkspaceFiles on DesktopApi.
details: Single shared event union imported by preload, renderer actions, and main.
FileEntry stays unchanged; the new subscription shape is additive.

nodes.title: store/actions.ts
nodes.summary: Hosts applyWorkspaceFileIndexEvent reducer and openWorkspace decoupling.
details: openWorkspace marks indexing and returns without awaiting the crawl, so loadPath can run.
Covered by store reducer tests for snapshot/upsert/remove and guard behavior.

nodes.title: electron/fileIndex.ts
nodes.summary: Index implementation: crawl, watch, batch deltas, recreate on ignore edits.
details: Depends on fileRules for crawl, ignore matching, markdown and ignore-config detection.
Covered by fileIndex.test.ts using temp directories.

nodes.title: electron/fileRules.ts
nodes.summary: Leaf: layered .gitignore/.ignore rules, hard ignores, markdown matching.
details: Wraps the `ignore` package with Git-like layered semantics and directory-only handling.
Covered by fileRules.test.ts for nested rules, negations, and hard ignores.

tour.title: Renderer entry point
tour.body: App.tsx is where the renderer subscribes to the workspace index and forwards events; it is the top of the renderer dependency chain.

tour.title: Main entry point
tour.body: main.ts is the other entry point: it owns the IPC handlers and constructs one index per watchId, delegating crawl logic downward.

tour.title: Index seam
tour.body: fileIndex.ts owns crawl-plus-watch behavior and is the major seam between IPC plumbing and the ignore rules it relies on.

tour.title: Leaf dependency
tour.body: fileRules.ts is the leaf: pure ignore and markdown logic with no Electron dependency, which is why it is unit-testable on its own.

tour.title: Shared contract
tour.body: types.ts defines the WorkspaceFileIndexEvent union that both entry points and the reducer depend on, keeping the boundary typed.

tour.title: Renderer reducer
tour.body: actions.ts consumes that contract to fold events into store state and to decouple opening a workspace from indexing it.

graphs.label: User action graph
graphs.summary: What the user does and how it flows: open a folder, get an immediately usable shell, watch the sidebar move from indexing to a ready tree, then see live incremental updates.

nodes.title: Open a folder
nodes.summary: User picks a Workspace or Plain Folder via Open Folder or the picker.
details: openWorkspace sets workspacePath, clears files, marks fileIndexStatus indexing, and returns.
The sidebar 'Open folder' button and CmdOrCtrl+Shift+N trigger the same path.

nodes.title: Shell stays usable
nodes.summary: Editor shell is interactive immediately; remembered/explicit files still load.
details: openWorkspace no longer awaits the crawl, so loadPath for a remembered or picked file runs during indexing.
Open File / Open Folder remain available while the sidebar indexes.

nodes.title: Sidebar shows indexing
nodes.summary: Sidebar renders a compact indexing status, not a partial tree.
details: SidebarStatus shows 'Indexing files / Building file tree...' while fileIndexStatus is indexing.
On error it shows a recoverable 'File tree unavailable' message.

nodes.title: Tree becomes ready
nodes.summary: Ready snapshot replaces the loading state with the markdown-only tree.
details: Expanding folders only reveals already-indexed paths; it does not trigger a new crawl.
An explicitly opened ignored file can stay open with no selected sidebar row.

nodes.title: Live edits update tree
nodes.summary: Adding, editing, deleting files or ignore rules updates the sidebar incrementally.
details: Markdown add/change/unlink and folder add/remove flow through batched deltas without full reload.
Editing .gitignore/.ignore recrawls and re-filters; unrelated non-markdown changes are ignored.

tour.title: User opens a folder
tour.body: The flow starts when the user opens a folder; the workspace path is set and indexing begins without blocking.

tour.title: Shell is usable right away
tour.body: Because opening no longer awaits the crawl, the editor shell stays interactive and a remembered or explicitly picked file can load during indexing.

tour.title: Sidebar indicates progress
tour.body: The sidebar shows a compact indexing state rather than a half-built tree, so the user knows discovery is in progress.

tour.title: Tree appears
tour.body: When the ready snapshot arrives, the sidebar swaps in the complete markdown-only tree; folder expansion is purely presentational.

tour.title: Live updates
tour.body: After that, file and ignore-rule changes update the sidebar incrementally, while unrelated non-markdown churn leaves it untouched.
